hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zpair_pos_med
candidate_specs.rationale: Current faithful12-base incumbent from the late11 fine-grid. Kept as the direct in-branch baseline for the Ka2_y attack.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_y10_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Explicit +Y / 0 / -Y / 0 late z-family exposure using two opposite closed z-pairs, with all dwell placed on ±Y and none on the intervening zero states.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_y10_x5
candidate_specs.rationale: Same bipolar ±Y pattern, but restore a small 5 s zero-state dwell between the positive and negative Y exposures to protect already-good channels.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_y15_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Heavier ±Y hold with zero-state dwell removed. Tests whether stronger sign-separated Y exposure lowers the Ka2_y ceiling further, even at some risk to the mean.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_d8_then_zbipolar_y10_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Mildly shorten the xpair outer holds before the zero-dwell bipolar z block. Tests whether more of the late budget should be shifted from xpair dwell into ±Y exposure.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_p10_x4_n10_x4
candidate_specs.rationale: Small-grid refinement around the successful bipolar family: reduce zero-state dwell from 5 s to 4 s.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_p10_x6_n10_x6
candidate_specs.rationale: Small-grid refinement around the successful bipolar family: increase zero-state dwell from 5 s to 6 s.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_p10_x5_n12_x5
candidate_specs.rationale: Asymmetric ±Y refinement: slightly heavier negative-Y dwell to compensate the base family’s existing positive-Y bias.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zbipolar_p8_x5_n12_x5
candidate_specs.rationale: Asymmetric ±Y refinement with a slightly lighter positive-Y hold and a heavier negative-Y hold.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Full four-quarter-turn late z-cycle after the xpair, with hold on ±Y only and no added dwell on the intermediate ±X states. This creates a continuity-safe +Y / -X / -Y / +X sweep while preserving z-family excitation.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y8_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Same four-quarter-turn z-cycle, but slightly lighter ±Y dwell to see whether the ceiling falls further when the cycle is tightened.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y12_x0
candidate_specs.rationale: Same four-quarter-turn z-cycle, but slightly heavier ±Y dwell to probe a nearby mean/max tradeoff point.

candidate_specs.name: late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x2
candidate_specs.rationale: Four-quarter-turn z-cycle with a small 2 s hold on the intermediate ±X states. This tests whether a little zero-state buffering stabilizes the full cycle while keeping the new ±Y structure.

bottom_line.ceiling_verdict: yes locally, but not fundamentally: the branch lowered the overall-best Ka2_y/max from 99.767 to 99.676, and the max-optimized sibling reached 99.609; however the gap to default18 max remains enormous (84.895 still open after only 0.158 points of closure).

scientific_conclusion: The focused late11 Ka2_y attack succeeded scientifically in two ways: (1) it found a new faithful12-base continuity-safe winner (late11_yneg_xpair_outerhold_then_zquad_y10_x2) at 10.600 / 1.369 / 99.676, which beats the old best legal non-faithful-base mean by 0.414 points; and (2) it lowered the Ka2_y ceiling modestly, with the max-optimized sibling reaching 99.609. But the ceiling is not truly broken: Ka2_y still remains the max channel and is still far above the default18 regime.
